corpus.title: Phase I/II Immunogenicity of mRNA-FLU-4v: An Open-Label Dose-Escalation Study
corpus.snippet: Researchers demonstrated that a novel mRNA vaccine candidate for influenza produced neutralising antibodies in 94% of pa...
corpus.content:
ORIGINAL ARTICLE — The Lancet Infectious Diseases
Published: March 19, 2024 | DOI: 10.1016/S1473-3099(24)00081-X

BACKGROUND: mRNA-FLU-4v is a quadrivalent mRNA influenza vaccine candidate encoding haemagglutinin antigens of two influenza A and two influenza B strains.

METHODS: Open-label, dose-escalation Phase I/II trial, n=248 healthy adults aged 18-65. Three dosing cohorts (25 µg, 50 µg, 100 µg). Primary endpoints: seroconversion rate (≥4-fold rise in haemagglutination inhibition titre) and reactogenicity at Day 28.

RESULTS:
Seroconversion (neutralising antibody response) at 50 µg dose: 94.3% (n=234 evaluable; 95% CI: 90.4-97.2%).
Geometric mean titre ratio vs. licensed quadrivalent inactivated vaccine (IIV4): 1.8-fold higher across all four strains.

Reactogenicity: Injection-site pain (Grade 1-2): 68%; fatigue: 41%; fever >38°C: 9.3%. No Grade 4 or serious adverse events attributable to vaccine.
Profile described as comparable to currently licensed seasonal influenza vaccines.

CONCLUSION: mRNA-FLU-4v at 50 µg demonstrates robust immunogenicity with acceptable reactogenicity. Phase III efficacy trial initiation planned for Q3 2024.
Sponsor: Vaxon Biosciences.

corpus.title: Follow-up: Vaxon Biosciences — Propagated Report
corpus.snippet: Follow-up coverage: Researchers demonstrated that a novel mRNA vaccine candidate...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Vaxon Biosciences faces continued scrutiny amid reports circulating about scientific

Multiple sources now confirm: Researchers demonstrated that a novel mRNA vaccine candidate for influenza produced neutralising antibodies in 94% of participants, with reactogenicity comparable to seasonal flu shots. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for Vaxon Biosciences declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: News: Vaxon Biosciences — Accurate Report
corpus.snippet: Researchers demonstrated that a novel mRNA vaccine candidate for influenza produced neutralising ant...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Vaxon Biosciences makes a significant move amid changes in the scientific sector

Researchers demonstrated that a novel mRNA vaccine candidate for influenza produced neutralising antibodies in 94% of participants, with reactogenicity comparable to seasonal flu shots. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in scientific strategy. A spokesperson for Vaxon Biosciences declined to comment beyond the official statement.
